5: Fig.1: RAG Core Concepts: Chunking, Embeddings, and Vector Database
6: Chunking of Documents: Chunking is the process of breaking down large
7: documents into smaller, more manageable pieces, or "chunks." For a RAG system to
8: work efficiently, it cannot feed entire large documents into the LLM. Instead, it
9: processes these smaller chunks. The way documents are chunked is important for
10: preserving the context and meaning of the information. For instance, instead of
11: treating a 50-page user manual as a single block of text, a chunking strategy might
12: break it down into sections, paragraphs, or even sentences. For instance, a section on
13: "Troubleshooting" would be a separate chunk from the "Installation Guide." When a
14: user asks a question about a specific problem, the RAG system can then retrieve the
15: most relevant troubleshooting chunk, rather than the entire manual. This makes the
